what_tested: Oral psilocybin (low and high dose) given in 3 sessions spaced 5 days apart, to see if it reduces cluster headache frequency and intensity.
key_result: Study completed in 2022 with 25 participants but no results have been posted yet. This was one of the first rigorous academic studies of psilocybin for CH.
patient_relevance: Yale's landmark psilocybin study completed but awaits publication -- results could shape future psychedelic treatment options for CH.
dose_tested: Low dose and high dose oral psilocybin (exact mg not specified), 3 sessions 5 days apart

what_tested: Microdose LSD (25 micrograms) given every 3 days for 3 weeks to treat chronic cluster headache, compared to placebo.
key_result: Currently recruiting 65 participants, expected to complete in 2027.
patient_relevance: If successful, this could establish microdose LSD as a legitimate treatment for chronic CH -- a disease with very few good prevention options.
dose_tested: 25 micrograms LSD every 3 days for 3 weeks

what_tested: Comparing high-flow and medium-flow oxygen therapy versus placebo for primary headache disorders in the emergency department.
key_result: Completed in 2022 with 132 participants but no results posted. Study focused on general primary headaches, not exclusively cluster headache.
patient_relevance: This may provide evidence on whether different oxygen flow rates matter for headache relief in the ER, though it was not CH-specific.

what_tested: Low-dose psilocybin given in 3 weekly sessions as a preventive treatment for chronic cluster headache, with brain imaging (fMRI) before and after.
key_result: Terminated after enrolling only 10 of planned participants, likely due to COVID-19 pandemic (started Jan 2020, ended Nov 2020).
patient_relevance: This Danish study was stopped early, probably due to COVID -- it does not tell us whether psilocybin works for chronic CH.
dose_tested: Low dose psilocybin, 3 sessions one week apart

what_tested: Intranasal ketamine spray (15mg per dose, up to 75mg total) as an acute rescue treatment for chronic cluster headache attacks.
key_result: Completed with 23 participants but no results posted. Based on the protocol, treatment was given during active attacks when pain exceeded 6/10.
patient_relevance: A nasal ketamine spray could be a fast-acting rescue option for CH attacks, but we are still waiting for results from this small study.
dose_tested: 15mg ketamine intranasal per dose, up to 5 doses (75mg total) over 24 minutes

what_tested: Oral LSD (100 micrograms) given 3 times over 3 weeks versus placebo in a crossover design, to prevent cluster headache attacks.
key_result: Still recruiting 30 participants as of 2025, expected completion December 2025.
patient_relevance: This Basel study tests a full psychoactive dose of LSD for CH prevention -- if it works, it could validate what many patients have reported anecdotally.
dose_tested: 100 micrograms oral LSD, 3 doses over 3 weeks

what_tested: Comparing three different oxygen delivery systems for acute treatment of cluster headache attacks.
key_result: Completed with 57 participants but no results posted.
patient_relevance: The type of oxygen mask or delivery system you use may matter for how well oxygen works -- this study could help answer that, but results are not yet available.
dose_tested: Three different oxygen delivery systems (specifics not detailed)

what_tested: Whether CPAP (continuous positive airway pressure, the machine used for sleep apnea) can prevent nocturnal cluster headache attacks.
key_result: Completed with 30 participants but no results posted. The idea was based on reports linking sleep apnea to cluster headache.
patient_relevance: If CPAP reduces nighttime CH attacks, it would be an affordable and widely available treatment -- but we are still waiting for results.

what_tested: A single IV infusion of ketamine combined with magnesium to reduce attacks in drug-resistant chronic cluster headache.
key_result: Still recruiting 90 participants, expected completion September 2026. Earlier pilot data in 17 patients showed daily attacks dropped from 4.3 to 1.3 (p<0.001).
patient_relevance: Promising pilot data suggests ketamine+magnesium IV could dramatically reduce attacks in treatment-resistant chronic CH -- this larger trial will tell us if it is real.
dose_tested: Single IV infusion of ketamine-magnesium combination

what_tested: Whether a demand-valve oxygen delivery system (which adjusts flow to breathing rate) works better than standard continuous-flow oxygen for aborting CH attacks.
key_result: Terminated after enrolling only 4 patients. Results section exists but contains no actual data. Zero adverse events reported.
patient_relevance: This study was too small to draw conclusions -- we still do not know if demand-valve oxygen is better than a standard non-rebreather mask.
dose_tested: Demand valve oxygen vs continuous high-flow oxygen via non-rebreather mask

what_tested: High-dose Vitamin D3 plus a multivitamin versus placebo for preventing cluster headache attacks.
key_result: Terminated after enrolling 27 of the planned participants. No results posted. The study ran from 2021 to 2024.
patient_relevance: Despite strong community interest in Vitamin D for CH, this rigorous trial was terminated early -- we still lack solid evidence on whether high-dose D3 prevents attacks.
dose_tested: High-dose Vitamin D3 + multivitamin (specific dose not stated)

what_tested: Botulinum toxin A (Botox) injected precisely into the sphenopalatine ganglion (SPG) using CT-guided navigation, to reduce attack frequency in treatment-resistant chronic CH.
key_result: Still recruiting 112 participants, expected completion September 2025. Earlier phase 1/2 data suggested the approach was safe and beneficial.
patient_relevance: If Botox injected at the SPG reduces chronic CH attacks, it could offer months of relief from a single injection for patients who have failed medications.
dose_tested: Botulinum toxin A single injection to sphenopalatine ganglion

what_tested: Long-term safety of galcanezumab (Emgality) 300mg monthly injection in people who completed the earlier episodic or chronic CH trials.
key_result: 119 of 165 participants experienced treatment-related adverse events, and 17 had serious adverse events. 2 participants reported suicidal ideation. No new safety signals beyond what was already known.
patient_relevance: Galcanezumab appears reasonably safe for long-term use in CH patients, though side effects are common -- this was a safety study, not designed to prove it works.

what_tested: Investigating whether specific microRNAs (small genetic molecules) in blood cells differ between CH patients in active phase, remission, and healthy controls, along with measuring CGRP levels.
key_result: Still recruiting 100 participants, expected completion December 2024.
patient_relevance: This is basic research that could lead to a blood test for CH or help identify new drug targets -- no direct treatment implications yet.

what_tested: Galcanezumab (Emgality) 300mg monthly injection versus placebo for preventing episodic cluster headache attacks.
key_result: Galcanezumab reduced weekly attacks by 8.69 compared to 5.22 for placebo (difference of ~3.5 fewer attacks per week). This was a statistically significant result that led to some clinical use.
patient_relevance: This is the study that made galcanezumab the first CGRP antibody to show benefit in episodic CH -- about 3-4 fewer attacks per week than placebo.

what_tested: Galcanezumab (Emgality) 300mg monthly injection versus placebo for preventing chronic cluster headache attacks.
key_result: Galcanezumab reduced weekly attacks by 5.38 versus 4.59 for placebo -- a difference of less than 1 attack per week, which was not statistically significant.
patient_relevance: Galcanezumab did NOT work for chronic CH -- the small improvement over placebo was not meaningful, which is disappointing since chronic patients need it most.

what_tested: Long-term (1 year) safety of eptinezumab IV infusion in chronic cluster headache patients.
key_result: 106 of 131 participants experienced adverse events, and 12 had serious adverse events. This was a safety extension study showing the drug was tolerable long-term.
patient_relevance: Eptinezumab appears safe enough for long-term use in chronic CH, but this study was not designed to prove effectiveness.
dose_tested: Eptinezumab IV infusion (dose not specified in summary)

what_tested: Eptinezumab (Vyepti) IV infusion versus placebo for preventing episodic cluster headache attacks.
key_result: Eptinezumab reduced weekly attacks by 4.0 versus 4.6 for placebo over weeks 1-2. The drug performed WORSE than placebo, meaning it completely failed its primary endpoint.
patient_relevance: Eptinezumab does not work for episodic CH -- placebo actually did better, which is a clear negative result.

what_tested: Rimegepant (Nurtec), a CGRP receptor blocker pill, as a preventive treatment for cluster headache.
key_result: Completed with 20 participants but no results posted yet.
patient_relevance: If an oral CGRP blocker works for CH prevention, it would be much more convenient than injections or infusions -- results are awaited.

what_tested: Erenumab (Aimovig), a CGRP receptor antibody, as a treatment for chronic cluster headache.
key_result: Completed with 101 participants but no results posted yet.
patient_relevance: Another CGRP antibody being tested specifically for chronic CH -- results could expand treatment options if positive, given galcanezumab failed for chronic CH.

what_tested: Endoscopic injection of long-acting bupivacaine (Exparel) near the sphenopalatine ganglion for craniofacial pain including cluster headache.
key_result: Still recruiting 15 participants, expected completion June 2025.
patient_relevance: A long-acting nerve block that could provide up to 72 hours of pain relief from a single injection -- early-stage and small but interesting.
dose_tested: Exparel (long-acting bupivacaine) endoscopic injection to lateral nasal wall

what_tested: Long-term safety and efficacy of fremanezumab (Ajovy) at various doses for preventing cluster headache (extension study of earlier trials).
key_result: Safety-focused study: adverse events occurred in most participants across all dose groups. No efficacy endpoints were primary. The parent trials were terminated, making this extension study less meaningful.
patient_relevance: Fremanezumab's entire CH program was discontinued -- this drug is not coming to market for cluster headache.

what_tested: Fremanezumab (Ajovy) at two dose levels versus placebo for preventing episodic cluster headache attacks.
key_result: The higher dose (900/225/225mg) reduced weekly attacks by 7.6 vs 5.7 for placebo, but the lower dose (675mg) showed essentially no difference from placebo (5.8 vs 5.7). Study was terminated.
patient_relevance: Fremanezumab failed for episodic CH -- the small signal in one dose group was not enough to continue development.

what_tested: Fremanezumab (Ajovy) at two dose levels versus placebo for preventing chronic cluster headache attacks.
key_result: Placebo reduced monthly attacks by 12.2, which was actually better than the lower fremanezumab dose (8.7). The higher dose (900/225/225mg) reduced attacks by 15.5 but the massive placebo response made results uninterpretable. Study terminated.
patient_relevance: Fremanezumab failed for chronic CH too -- the huge placebo response (12 fewer monthly attacks) wiped out any signal from the drug.

what_tested: Occipital nerve stimulation (ONS) via implanted electrodes at the back of the head, comparing high (100%) vs low (30%) stimulation intensity for treatment-resistant chronic cluster headache.
key_result: Completed with 130 participants but no results posted. This was the largest randomized controlled trial of ONS for chronic CH.
patient_relevance: ONS is already used clinically for refractory CH in some countries -- this major trial should clarify how effective it really is, but results are not yet public.
dose_tested: Occipital nerve stimulation at 100% vs 30% intensity

what_tested: Repeated greater occipital nerve (GON) steroid injections versus placebo as a preventive treatment for chronic cluster headache.
key_result: Still recruiting 50 participants, expected completion December 2025.
patient_relevance: GON injections are already used by many headache specialists -- this trial will finally tell us if repeated injections provide lasting benefit for chronic CH.
dose_tested: Repeated GON injections (steroid, specific agent not detailed)

what_tested: Whether opening ATP-sensitive potassium channels (using levcromakalim) triggers cluster headache attacks, to understand the underlying disease mechanism.
key_result: Completed with 41 participants but no results posted. This was a provocation study, not a treatment trial.
patient_relevance: This is basic science research -- if potassium channels trigger CH attacks, drugs that block them could become future treatments.

what_tested: Whether IV infusion of CGRP (the protein targeted by new migraine drugs) can trigger cluster headache attacks in patients during active and remission phases.
key_result: Completed with 37 participants but no results posted. This provocation study tested the role of CGRP in triggering CH.
patient_relevance: Understanding whether CGRP directly causes CH attacks helps explain why some CGRP drugs work and others do not -- foundational research.

what_tested: Pulsed radiofrequency (PRF) targeting the sphenopalatine ganglion (SPG) as a minimally invasive treatment for chronic cluster headache.
key_result: Recruiting 108 participants, expected completion December 2029.
patient_relevance: SPG pulsed radiofrequency is a minimally invasive procedure that avoids permanent nerve damage -- this large trial could establish it as a standard option for chronic CH.

what_tested: Transcranial direct current stimulation (tDCS) -- a gentle electrical brain stimulation applied to the forehead -- as a preventive treatment for chronic cluster headache.
key_result: Completed with 32 participants but no results posted.
patient_relevance: tDCS is non-invasive and could be done at home if effective -- but without published results we cannot know if it helps CH.

what_tested: Non-invasive vagus nerve stimulation (nVNS) for acute treatment of primary headache in children and adolescents aged 7-20.
key_result: Recruiting 15 participants, expected completion August 2026. This is primarily a migraine study in young patients, not specifically CH.
patient_relevance: Mostly relevant to pediatric migraine, but could provide some data on vagus nerve stimulation in young headache patients generally.
dose_tested: Non-invasive vagus nerve stimulation via ear-mounted device

what_tested: Pulsed radiofrequency combined with platelet-rich plasma (PRP) injected at the sphenopalatine ganglion for episodic cluster headache.
key_result: Not yet recruiting, expected to enroll 242 participants starting May 2025.
patient_relevance: Combining two minimally invasive techniques could potentially extend pain relief duration -- a novel approach worth watching.
dose_tested: Pulsed radiofrequency + platelet-rich plasma at SPG

what_tested: Safety of Botox injected into the otic ganglion (a different nerve cluster than the usual SPG target) in chronic cluster headache patients.
key_result: Completed with 10 participants. This was a phase 1/2 safety pilot testing 12.5 and 25 IU botulinum toxin. No results posted.
patient_relevance: Early safety study exploring a new injection target for CH -- too small to tell if it works, but establishes whether the approach is safe.
dose_tested: 12.5 IU and 25 IU botulinum toxin A to otic ganglion

what_tested: Safety of Botox injected into the sphenopalatine ganglion (SPG) using CT-guided navigation for treatment-resistant cluster headache.
key_result: Completed with 10 participants. This was a phase 1/2 safety and feasibility study testing 25 IU and 50 IU doses. No results posted.
patient_relevance: This Norwegian pilot established the safety of SPG Botox injection for CH, paving the way for the larger phase 3 trial (NCT03944876).
dose_tested: 25 IU and 50 IU botulinum toxin A to sphenopalatine ganglion

what_tested: A tiny implantable nerve stimulator (bion microstimulator) placed near the greater occipital nerve to treat chronic headaches including cluster headache.
key_result: Completed with 20 participants but no results posted. This was one of the earliest occipital nerve stimulation studies for headache.
patient_relevance: An early study of implantable nerve stimulation for chronic headache -- the technology has evolved significantly since this 2004-2006 trial.
dose_tested: Bion microstimulator implanted at greater occipital nerve

what_tested: The PRIMUS system combining supraorbital nerve stimulation (SONS) and occipital nerve stimulation (ONS) for chronic cluster headache -- a first-in-human study.
key_result: Active but no longer recruiting, with 5 participants. Expected completion April 2024.
patient_relevance: A novel dual-site stimulation system that may work better than stimulating just one nerve -- very early stage with only 5 patients.
dose_tested: PRIMUS combined SONS + ONS neurostimulation system

what_tested: Using capsaicin (hot pepper extract) and ice water to study pain biomarkers in headache patients, and testing whether oxygen reduces capsaicin-induced eye watering to understand how oxygen therapy works.
key_result: Enrolling by invitation, with 371 participants. Expected completion May 2028. This is a mechanism/biomarker study.
patient_relevance: This research could explain WHY oxygen works for CH -- understanding the mechanism could lead to better treatments.

what_tested: Low sodium oxybate (Xywav), a sleep medication, taken twice nightly to prevent nocturnal cluster headache attacks in chronic CH.
key_result: Not yet recruiting, expected to start January 2026 with 52 participants.
patient_relevance: Many CH attacks happen at night -- a medication that both improves sleep and prevents nocturnal attacks could be very valuable for CH patients.

what_tested: GammaCore (non-invasive vagus nerve stimulator) applied to the neck versus sham device for aborting acute cluster headache attacks.
key_result: GammaCore showed 67 pain-free attacks out of 495 treated (13.5%) versus 46 out of 400 for sham (11.5%). The difference was modest and this trial had mixed results across episodic and chronic subtypes.
patient_relevance: GammaCore showed a small benefit for acute CH attacks overall, but the advantage over sham was not dramatic -- it works better for episodic than chronic CH.
dose_tested: GammaCore non-invasive vagus nerve stimulation device

what_tested: An implanted neurostimulator to electrically stimulate the sphenopalatine ganglion (SPG) for acute treatment of cluster headache attacks.
key_result: Completed with 43 participants. The Pathway CH-1 study showed SPG stimulation provided pain relief in 67% of attacks and complete pain freedom in 34% within 15 minutes.
patient_relevance: SPG stimulation was highly effective for aborting CH attacks -- this led to the Pulsante device being available in Europe, though the company later closed.
dose_tested: SPG neurostimulation via implanted ATI device

what_tested: Light therapy (bright light delivered via consumer device) as a preventive treatment for chronic cluster headache, based on CH being a circadian rhythm disorder.
key_result: Recruiting 48 participants, expected completion February 2027.
patient_relevance: If light therapy helps reset the circadian rhythm disruption in CH, it would be a simple, side-effect-free addition to treatment -- especially for patients with seasonal patterns.

what_tested: GammaCore (non-invasive vagus nerve stimulator) versus sham for acute treatment of cluster headache -- the pivotal regulatory trial.
key_result: 16 of 44 GammaCore patients (36%) were responders versus 14 of 57 sham patients (25%). In the episodic CH subgroup, response rate was significantly higher with GammaCore (48% vs 6%). Chronic CH showed no significant difference.
patient_relevance: GammaCore works for aborting episodic CH attacks but NOT for chronic CH -- it is now FDA-cleared based partly on this data, but benefit is limited to episodic patients.

what_tested: Post-market registry collecting real-world data on the ATI SPG neurostimulation system implant for cluster headache.
key_result: Completed with 150 participants. No formal results posted, but this registry supported the commercial use of the Pulsante device in Europe.
patient_relevance: Real-world data collection on the SPG stimulator -- the device showed promise but the company (Autonomic Technologies) eventually closed down.

what_tested: Retrospective evaluation of sphenopalatine ganglion pulsed radiofrequency treatment outcomes for chronic headaches and facial pain, tracking pain scores over 6 months.
key_result: Completed with 55 participants but no results posted yet. Data collected at 1 week, 1 month, 3 months, and 6 months.
patient_relevance: Real-world outcomes data on SPG radiofrequency could help patients decide whether this minimally invasive procedure is worth trying.

what_tested: The PRIMUS system combining trigeminal and occipital nerve stimulation for preventing chronic cluster headache in patients who failed verapamil.
key_result: Recruiting 10 participants at two centers in the Netherlands, expected completion April 2027.
patient_relevance: A next-generation neurostimulation device for CH patients who cannot tolerate verapamil -- still very early stage.
dose_tested: PRIMUS combined trigeminal + occipital nerve stimulation

what_tested: Sphenopalatine ganglion pulsed radiofrequency for treating cluster headache -- effectiveness and safety.
key_result: Completed with 80 participants but no results posted.
patient_relevance: A reasonably large study of SPG radiofrequency for CH from a major Chinese center -- results would be valuable but have not been published.

what_tested: Repetitive transcranial magnetic stimulation (rTMS) versus sham stimulation for refractory chronic cluster headache -- 10 sessions over 2 weeks in a crossover design.
key_result: Completed with 8 participants but no results posted. Very small pilot study.
patient_relevance: TMS is non-invasive and already used for depression -- if it works for refractory CH, it could help the hardest-to-treat patients, but this was very small.
dose_tested: rTMS for 10 minutes, 10 consecutive working days

what_tested: Whether occipital nerve stimulator (ONS) implantation changes pain processing and biomarkers in chronic migraine and cluster headache patients.
key_result: Completed with 6 participants. This was a mechanistic study comparing pain perception before and after ONS implantation.
patient_relevance: Understanding how ONS changes pain processing could help predict which patients will respond -- basic science, not a treatment trial.

what_tested: Comparing two ultrasound-guided techniques for greater occipital nerve (GON) blocks -- a proximal approach at the C2 vertebra versus a distal approach at the base of the skull.
key_result: Completed with 40 participants but no results posted.
patient_relevance: How your doctor performs a GON block may affect how well it works -- this study compared two common injection sites.
dose_tested: GON block with local anesthetic and steroid, two different injection sites

what_tested: Whether low-frequency electrical stimulation of the sphenopalatine ganglion (SPG) can trigger cluster-like attacks by activating parasympathetic nerves.
key_result: Completed with 21 participants but no results posted. This was a provocation study to understand CH mechanisms.
patient_relevance: Basic science study to understand how the SPG contributes to CH attacks -- helps explain why SPG-targeted treatments work.

what_tested: Deep brain stimulation (DBS) with electrodes implanted in the posterior hypothalamus for treatment-resistant chronic cluster headache, in a crossover design with stimulation on vs off.
key_result: Completed with 12 participants but no results posted. Efficacy defined as 50% or more reduction in weekly attacks.
patient_relevance: DBS is an extreme last-resort treatment requiring brain surgery -- it is only for patients who have failed everything else, and results from this controlled study would be very informative.
dose_tested: Deep brain stimulation of posterior-inferior hypothalamus

what_tested: Whether cluster headache patients experience cutaneous allodynia (pain from normally non-painful touch) during and between attacks.
key_result: 20 out of 41 CH patients showed allodynia (about 49%), demonstrating that nearly half of CH patients have abnormal pain sensitivity on the skin.
patient_relevance: Nearly half of CH patients have skin sensitivity (allodynia) -- this suggests central sensitization occurs in CH, similar to migraine, which could affect treatment choices.

what_tested: Deep brain stimulation of the third ventricle area for both chronic cluster headache and obesity -- exploring shared hypothalamic pathways.
key_result: Completed with 13 participants but no results posted. Study ran for over 10 years.
patient_relevance: An unusual study targeting a different brain area than typical DBS for CH -- results could reveal connections between CH and metabolic regulation.
dose_tested: Deep brain stimulation of the third ventricle

what_tested: Long-term follow-up of patients from the Pathway CH-1 trial who had SPG neurostimulators implanted for cluster headache.
key_result: Completed with 33 participants. This tracked the safety and effectiveness of SPG stimulation over years of use.
patient_relevance: Long-term data on SPG stimulation is critical for patients considering an implant -- suggests the device continues to work over time.

what_tested: How SPG stimulation at different frequencies affects blood flow in the brain -- high frequency was expected to block parasympathetic activity, low frequency to activate it.
key_result: Completed with 7 participants. This was a mechanistic study measuring blood vessel responses to different stimulation patterns.
patient_relevance: Understanding how SPG stimulation changes blood flow helps explain why it can both abort attacks and prevent them -- important basic science.
dose_tested: SPG stimulation at various frequencies (1-200 Hz)

what_tested: Web-based or face-to-face yoga-based movement program as a complementary treatment for migraine and/or cluster headache, focusing on sleep quality and mental health.
key_result: Recruiting 40 participants, expected completion May 2026. This is a pilot feasibility study, not primarily CH-specific.
patient_relevance: Yoga may help with sleep and wellbeing for CH patients, but this is a feasibility study focused mainly on migraine -- do not expect breakthrough CH results.

what_tested: Cortivazol (a corticosteroid) injected at the greater occipital nerve as an add-on to verapamil for preventing cluster headache attacks.
key_result: Completed with 43 participants but no results posted.
patient_relevance: Steroid injections at the greater occipital nerve are widely used for CH -- this study tested whether they add benefit to verapamil.
dose_tested: Cortivazol injection at greater occipital nerve + oral verapamil

what_tested: A three-phase study testing TENS (transcutaneous electrical nerve stimulation), then randomizing to either burst occipital nerve stimulation or sham, then open-label conventional ONS for chronic cluster headache.
key_result: Completed with 38 participants but no results posted.
patient_relevance: This study tested both non-invasive (TENS) and implanted (ONS) nerve stimulation for chronic CH -- results could help decide between these options.
dose_tested: TENS then burst ONS vs sham ONS then tonic ONS

what_tested: Randomized controlled trial of web-based or face-to-face yoga for migraine and/or cluster headache versus conventional treatment.
key_result: Not yet recruiting, expected to start March 2026 with 80 participants. This is the full RCT following the pilot study.
patient_relevance: If yoga significantly improves quality of life for CH patients, it could be a low-risk complementary approach -- but this is mainly a migraine study.

what_tested: Intra-arterial delivery of dexamethasone and ketorolac into the arteries feeding the sphenopalatine ganglion for refractory headaches including cluster headache.
key_result: Withdrawn before enrolling any participants. The study never got off the ground.
patient_relevance: This novel approach of delivering medication directly to the SPG through blood vessels was never tested -- the concept remains unproven.
dose_tested: Intra-arterial dexamethasone + ketorolac to SPG arteries

what_tested: Whether eye-tracking measurements (saccadometry) show differences in brain function between migraine, cluster headache, and healthy people.
key_result: Terminated after enrolling 42 participants. This was a diagnostic/mechanism study, not a treatment trial.
patient_relevance: An attempt to find brain function differences in CH using eye-tracking -- terminated before completion, so no useful conclusions.

what_tested: A novel bilateral occipital nerve field stimulation device (BliStim) for preventing chronic cluster headaches -- first-in-human safety study.
key_result: Suspended with 10 participants enrolled. Reasons for suspension not stated.
patient_relevance: A new type of nerve stimulation device for CH, but the study was suspended -- unclear if it will resume.
dose_tested: BliStim bilateral occipital nerve field stimulation

what_tested: Brain imaging (PET scan) study to precisely locate hypothalamic activation during spontaneous cluster headache attacks.
key_result: Terminated after enrolling only 1 participant. Capturing a spontaneous attack during a brain scan is extremely difficult.
patient_relevance: The hypothalamus is believed to be the "generator" of CH -- but imaging attacks in real-time proved nearly impossible in this study.

what_tested: Monitoring the cardiac side effects (specifically heart block) of high-dose verapamil, the standard first-line preventive for cluster headache.
key_result: Active but no longer recruiting, with 60 participants. Expected completion September 2025.
patient_relevance: Verapamil can cause dangerous heart rhythm problems at the high doses needed for CH -- this study quantifies that risk and could lead to better monitoring guidelines.
dose_tested: Verapamil at CH treatment doses (higher than cardiology doses)

what_tested: R-verapamil (the right-handed version of the molecule, which may have fewer heart side effects) for preventing episodic cluster headache.
key_result: Terminated after enrolling only 1 participant out of planned enrollment. The study effectively failed to recruit.
patient_relevance: A safer version of verapamil would be very welcome for CH patients, but this trial never got enough participants to test the idea.

what_tested: Greater occipital nerve (GON) steroid injection as a FIRST-LINE preventive treatment for episodic cluster headache, potentially replacing the need for verapamil.
key_result: Completed with 70 participants but no results posted.
patient_relevance: If GON injection works as well as verapamil for starting a new cluster period, patients could avoid weeks of pill-taking and cardiac monitoring -- results eagerly awaited.
dose_tested: GON steroid injection as first-line prophylaxis

what_tested: Using actigraphy (wrist activity monitors) and heart rate variability to study the relationship between sleep, autonomic nervous system function, and cluster headache attacks.
key_result: Completed with 20 participants but no results posted. This was an observational study comparing bout and remission phases.
patient_relevance: Understanding the sleep-autonomic-headache connection could lead to better timing of preventive medications or sleep-based interventions.

what_tested: A massive long-term registry collecting clinical data, biomarkers, imaging, genetics, and treatment outcomes for headache and vertigo patients in China.
key_result: Recruiting, with 10,000 participants enrolled. Runs until 2050.
patient_relevance: This huge database may eventually reveal patterns about CH that smaller studies miss, but it is a data collection effort, not testing a treatment.

what_tested: Studying psychological factors (personality traits, defense mechanisms, trauma history) in women with chronic migraine, fibromyalgia, and vulvodynia.
key_result: Completed with 1,006 participants. This was not a cluster headache study.
patient_relevance: Not directly relevant to CH -- this studied psychological factors in other chronic pain conditions.

what_tested: A long-term observational research network collecting data on cluster headache and trigeminal neuralgia patients at the Will Erwin Headache Research Center.
key_result: Recruiting, with 1,500 participants. Runs until 2035.
patient_relevance: This US-based research network is building the largest CH patient database -- it supports multiple sub-studies and could accelerate treatment discoveries.

what_tested: Topiramate (Topamax), an anti-seizure medication, rapidly titrated to try to shorten cluster periods and prevent attacks.
key_result: Terminated with 60 participants enrolled but no results posted.
patient_relevance: Topiramate is sometimes used off-label for CH, but this controlled trial was terminated without providing clear evidence either way.

what_tested: A large online survey asking CH patients about treatment effectiveness and their views on various treatments.
key_result: Completed with 2,487 participants -- one of the largest CH surveys ever conducted.
patient_relevance: Patient-reported data on what actually works in real life -- this survey captures the CH community's experience with treatments, complementing clinical trial data.

what_tested: Using natural language processing (AI) to analyze how headache patients describe their condition in writing, to potentially improve diagnosis.
key_result: Completed with 1,150 participants.
patient_relevance: AI analysis of patient descriptions could someday help diagnose CH faster -- the average CH patient waits years for a correct diagnosis.

what_tested: Detailed study of sleep patterns and circadian (24-hour body clock) rhythms in episodic cluster headache patients, comparing in-bout versus out-of-bout phases.
key_result: Completed with 8 participants. Very small but detailed physiological study.
patient_relevance: CH attacks follow circadian patterns -- understanding the sleep and body-clock changes could lead to chronotherapy (timing-based treatment) approaches.

what_tested: Modified Atkins ketogenic diet (very low carbohydrate) as a treatment for chronic cluster headache, based on its success in epilepsy and preliminary migraine data.
key_result: Completed with 18 participants. No formal results posted, but published research from this group showed encouraging reductions in attack frequency.
patient_relevance: The ketogenic diet is something patients can try on their own -- early signals suggest it may help some CH patients, possibly through anti-inflammatory effects.

what_tested: Whether breathing techniques can reduce migraine attack frequency and severity when added to standard medication.
key_result: Completed with 86 participants. This was a migraine study, not a cluster headache study.
patient_relevance: Not directly relevant to CH -- breathing techniques were tested for migraine only.
dose_tested: Breathing techniques as add-on to migraine medication

what_tested: Using wearable sensors and machine learning to detect stress, behavior changes, and predict headache attacks in migraine and cluster headache patients.
key_result: Completed with 19 participants. Pilot study for wearable-based headache prediction.
patient_relevance: If a smartwatch could predict an incoming CH attack, patients could treat preemptively -- this pilot explored whether that is technically feasible.

what_tested: Intranasal civamide (zucapsaicin), a capsaicin-related compound, as a preventive nasal spray for episodic cluster headache.
key_result: Completed with only 2 participants out of planned enrollment. Effectively failed to recruit.
patient_relevance: A capsaicin-like nasal spray could theoretically desensitize the trigeminal nerve, but this study was far too small to tell us anything.

what_tested: A zolmitriptan microneedle skin patch (C213) applied during acute cluster headache attacks versus placebo, at 1.9mg and 3.8mg doses.
key_result: Pain relief at 15 minutes: placebo 3 patients, C213 1.9mg 5 patients, C213 3.8mg 5 patients. Sustained pain relief was similarly close across groups. With only 42 total participants and small numbers per group, no clear separation from placebo.
patient_relevance: A needle-free triptan skin patch would be convenient, but this small study did not show a clear advantage over placebo for aborting CH attacks.

what_tested: Self-injection of sumatriptan 3mg (half the standard Western dose) for aborting migraine and cluster headache attacks in Japan.
key_result: 93.9% of cluster headache patients achieved headache relief at 30 minutes post-injection. This was a non-comparative study confirming self-injection feasibility in Japan.
patient_relevance: Sumatriptan injection remains one of the most effective acute CH treatments -- even at the lower 3mg dose used in Japan, it works in over 90% of attacks.

what_tested: Intranasal civamide (zucapsaicin) nasal solution versus placebo for treating episodic cluster headache over 49 days.
key_result: Completed with 60 participants but no results posted.
patient_relevance: The larger of two civamide studies for CH -- capsaicin-based nasal sprays remain an unproven but theoretically interesting approach.

what_tested: A nurse-led telephone follow-up program for headache patients starting preventive treatment, compared to standard GP follow-up.
key_result: Recruiting 400 participants, expected completion December 2026.
patient_relevance: Better follow-up could improve how well patients stick with their CH preventive medications -- not a drug trial but could improve care quality.

what_tested: A survey studying barriers to clinical trial participation among cluster headache patients, particularly in underrepresented demographic groups.
key_result: Not yet recruiting, planned for 500 participants.
patient_relevance: CH trials often struggle to recruit enough patients -- understanding barriers could make future trials faster and more inclusive.

what_tested: Ausanil (an intranasal product) for primary headache disorders including cluster headache -- an observational safety and tolerability registry.
key_result: Terminated with 20 participants. No results posted.
patient_relevance: Ausanil was a commercial nasal product marketed for headache relief -- this registry was terminated and the treatment has not gained traction.

what_tested: Studying molecular, physiological, and emotional effects of intensive meditation in a large group including both healthy people and chronic pain patients.
key_result: Enrolling by invitation with 3,000 participants. Not specific to CH.
patient_relevance: Meditation may have general benefits for chronic pain sufferers, but this is not a CH-specific study.

what_tested: GammaCore (non-invasive vagus nerve stimulator) for both prevention and acute treatment of chronic cluster headache versus standard of care.
key_result: GammaCore reduced CH attacks by 7.6 per week versus 2.0 per week with standard of care alone -- a significant improvement. This was one of the more positive CH results for the device.
patient_relevance: GammaCore showed meaningful benefit for chronic CH prevention in this study -- about 5 fewer attacks per week compared to standard treatment alone.

what_tested: Candesartan (an angiotensin receptor blocker blood pressure medication) as a preventive treatment for episodic cluster headache.
key_result: Completed with 40 participants but no results posted.
patient_relevance: Candesartan has shown some promise for migraine prevention -- if it works for CH too, it would be a cheap, widely available option with fewer side effects than verapamil.

what_tested: Whether asking primary care doctors to commit to Choosing Wisely guidelines reduces unnecessary imaging tests for headaches.
key_result: Completed with 45 clinicians. This was about reducing unnecessary tests, not about CH treatment.
patient_relevance: Not relevant to CH treatment -- this was about healthcare quality improvement for general headache management.

what_tested: Studying the relationship between cluster headache, addictive behaviors, and blood vessel function (endothelial function).
key_result: Completed with 95 participants. Observational study exploring vascular and addiction links in CH.
patient_relevance: Understanding vascular dysfunction in CH could explain why the condition runs in cycles and may help identify at-risk individuals.

what_tested: Whether a neuroma (nerve tumor) of the zygomatic-temporal nerve could be triggering cluster headache, with surgical exploration and removal.
key_result: Completed with 5 participants. A highly exploratory study testing a novel theory about CH causation.
patient_relevance: A very unconventional hypothesis -- if a specific nerve tumor triggers CH in some patients, surgery could cure them, but this is highly speculative.

what_tested: Whether osteopathic manipulative therapy (OMT) changes brain structure and function in primary headache patients, measured by brain imaging.
key_result: Completed with 31 participants. This examined brain changes from manual therapy, not CH-specific.
patient_relevance: Osteopathic manipulation is unlikely to treat CH specifically, but understanding how manual therapies affect the pain-processing brain could have broader implications.

what_tested: L-cysteine (sold as Acetium capsules) taken daily to prevent or abort cluster headache attacks, based on its ability to neutralize acetaldehyde.
key_result: Completed with 60 participants but no results posted.
patient_relevance: L-cysteine is an over-the-counter supplement -- if it works, it would be an accessible and cheap option for CH prevention, but we lack published evidence.

what_tested: IV Depacon (valproate) for 2-3 days followed by oral Depakote ER (extended-release valproate) to quickly break a cluster headache cycle.
key_result: Average time to achieve 50% reduction in attack frequency or severity was 29.7 days (SD 13.8). This is not impressively fast for a treatment intended to provide rapid relief.
patient_relevance: IV valproate followed by oral Depakote took about a month to halve attack burden -- this is not much faster than standard verapamil titration, limiting its practical advantage.
dose_tested: 1000mg IV Depacon + 1000mg oral Depakote ER daily, then 500-2000mg Depakote ER maintenance

what_tested: Sleep study comparing sleep parameters in episodic cluster headache patients during bouts versus between bouts.
key_result: Completed with 66 participants. Observational sleep research.
patient_relevance: Understanding how sleep differs during CH bouts could explain why attacks cluster at night and might lead to sleep-targeted treatments.

what_tested: Investigating the role of hydration and fluid intake in children and adolescents with primary headaches.
key_result: Completed with 60 participants. Pediatric study, not CH-specific.
patient_relevance: Primarily relevant to pediatric headache -- dehydration is a known headache trigger but this is not specific to cluster headache.

what_tested: Personalized psychological intervention (CBT and ACT-based) for adolescents with headaches, delivered in 4-5 online sessions.
key_result: Completed with 5 participants. A small single-case experimental design study for pediatric headache.
patient_relevance: Psychological coping skills may help young headache patients but this tiny study is not specifically about CH.

what_tested: Intranasal cooling (spraying a coolant into the nose to cool blood vessels supplying the brain) for aborting migraine and cluster headache attacks.
key_result: Completed with 15 participants (5 with CH). Each patient received 10 treatments of up to 20 minutes. No results posted.
patient_relevance: Cooling the blood vessels in the nose is a creative approach -- if it works, it would be drug-free and fast-acting, but results are unknown.
dose_tested: Intranasal evaporative cooling, up to 20 minutes per treatment

what_tested: Nasal carbon dioxide (CO2) delivered by a handheld device to abort cluster headache attacks.
key_result: Terminated with 10 participants. Average pain reduction was -0.826 on a 0-3 scale (about 28% improvement). No serious adverse events. Study too small for conclusions.
patient_relevance: Nasal CO2 showed modest pain reduction but the study was too small and had no placebo control -- not enough evidence to recommend it for CH.
dose_tested: Nasal CO2 at 0.5 standard liters per minute, 10 seconds per nostril, up to 6 doses

what_tested: Three-month wearable sensor study using machine learning to predict headache attacks and track stress, sleep, and activity in migraine and cluster headache patients.
key_result: Completed with 30 participants. Technology development study.
patient_relevance: Building toward smartwatch-based CH attack prediction -- could eventually enable preemptive treatment if the technology matures.

what_tested: Retrospective analysis of regional anesthetic infiltration techniques for chronic pain syndromes including refractory headache.
key_result: Completed with 83 participants. Retrospective data analysis of pain scores during treatment.
patient_relevance: Local anesthetic infiltration is used by some pain clinics for refractory CH -- this retrospective data may add to the evidence base.

what_tested: Studying risk-taking and decision-making behavior in cluster headache patients to understand potential shared biology with addictive tendencies.
key_result: Completed with 140 participants. Behavioral/psychological research.
patient_relevance: CH patients may have different reward-seeking behavior -- understanding this could shed light on shared hypothalamic pathways between CH and addiction.

what_tested: Using actigraphy (wrist activity monitors) to study circadian rhythm and attack timing in episodic cluster headache during and between bouts.
key_result: Completed with 60 participants. Published research confirmed the strong circadian pattern of CH attacks.
patient_relevance: Confirmed what patients know: CH attacks follow the body clock -- this supports chronotherapy approaches and circadian-based treatments.

what_tested: SOM230 (pasireotide), a somatostatin analogue, injected subcutaneously at 1.5mg to abort acute cluster headache attacks versus placebo.
key_result: Headache response (pain dropping to mild/none at 30 min): 10 out of treated patients on SOM230 versus 9 on placebo -- essentially no difference. Study terminated.
patient_relevance: Pasireotide did not work for aborting CH attacks -- it performed no better than placebo injection.

what_tested: Whether a specific gene variant (SPINK-1) is associated with cluster headache, building on earlier genetic findings.
key_result: Completed with 42 participants. Genetic association study.
patient_relevance: Finding genetic links to CH could eventually lead to targeted treatments or early identification of at-risk individuals.

what_tested: Studying autonomic nervous system variability during cluster headache attacks to determine if autonomic dysfunction triggers or results from attacks.
key_result: Terminated with 19 participants.
patient_relevance: Understanding whether autonomic dysfunction causes or follows CH attacks is fundamental -- but this study was terminated before answering the question.

what_tested: Comparing droperidol versus metoclopramide + diphenhydramine for emergency department treatment of primary headaches.
key_result: Terminated with 19 participants. Results section exists but contains no actual pain score data. Not CH-specific.
patient_relevance: Not specifically relevant to CH -- this was about ER headache treatment in general and was terminated early.
